Items.Title: Lambda Powertools TypeScript 正式发布 | Lambda Powertools TypeScript is Generally Available
Items.Description:
早在 2022 年 1 月，我就第一次看到了Lambda Powertools TypeScript 。当时我对这个库感到非常兴奋，但它伴随着警告说它还没有准备好用于生产。 好吧，一般可用性公告于 7 月 15 日发布，所以是时候再看看了。

目录

发生了什么变化

那么测试版有什么变化呢？ 看一眼CHANGELOG就知道答案是六个月内变化不大。 它仍然涵盖了日志记录、度量和跟踪的核心能力，并且没有添加新的能力。 除了一些错误修复和优化之外，这仍然是我在一月份预览的库。

我可能希望看到的一项更改是对ES Modules的支持。 无服务器社区对 ES 模块的兴趣一直在迅速增长，这主要是由于Top-Level Await使用的愿望。

目前，可以使用require shim或通过cjs 技巧来解决问题，但很高兴看到 Lambda Powertools TypeScript 中对 ES 模块的原生支持。

比较

鉴于我已经在上一篇文章中介绍了这些模块，我想我应该将 Lambda Powertools TypeScript 模块与类似的解决方案进行比较。 我正在查看 API、捆绑的脚本大小、冷启动和执行时间。 为了收集指标，我使用 Step Functions 编写了一个小应用程序，它可以并行运行函数的许多实例并捕获指标。

我的基准测试工具将运行每个函数 50 次，目标是达到 20% 的冷启动率。 代码示例可在GitHub 上获得。

记录器

从 AWS Lambda 登录到 CloudWatch 的最简单方法是使用console.log 。 这样做不会使您的函数变得臃肿，没有依赖项管理，并且对 CloudWatch 的写入是异步的，因此操作是非阻塞的。 许多开发人员使用库来保证日志采用结构化格式并控制日志的详细程度。

也就是说，我们可以使用console作为基准，因为它很简单。 如果我们想要一个简单地写入一些非结构化日志的函数，我们可以这样做。

 import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , APIGatewayProxyResultV2 , Context , } from ' aws-lambda ' ; export const handler = async ( event : APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , context : Context ): Promise < APIGatewayProxyResultV2 > => { console . log ( ' event: ' , event ); console . log ( ' context: ' , context ); return { statusCode : 200 }; };

注销事件会产生字符串化的上下文对象：

 2022-07-19T12:10:45.503Z 2abe532e-2b26-46b5-9a65-884363160556 INFO context: { callbackWaitsForEmptyEventLoop: [Getter/Setter], succeed: [Function (anonymous)], fail: [Function (anonymous)], done: [Function (anonymous)], functionVersion: '$LATEST', functionName: 'LoggerConsole', memoryLimitInMB: '128', logGroupName: '/aws/lambda/LoggerConsole', logStreamName: '2022/07/19/[$LATEST]384dbd25ffeb4af49bc22c2ac4f333df', clientContext: undefined, identity: undefined, invokedFunctionArn: 'arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456790:function:LoggerConsole', awsRequestId: '2abe532e-2b26-46b5-9a65-884363160556', getRemainingTimeInMillis: [Function: getRemainingTimeInMillis] }

这是相当嘈杂的，让那些成功、失败和完成的方法并没有提供太多价值。

使用 Powertools，我们可以将更有用的上下文注入到日志消息中。

 import { Logger } from ' @aws-lambda-powertools/logger ' ; import type { LambdaInterface } from ' @aws-lambda-powertools/commons ' ; import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , Context } from ' aws-lambda ' ; const logger = new Logger (); class Lambda implements LambdaInterface { @ logger . injectLambdaContext ({ logEvent : true }) public async handler ( _event : APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , _context : Context ): Promise < void > { logger . info ( ' Here is some info! ' ); } } export const myFunction = new Lambda (); export const handler = myFunction . handler ;

现在我们得到结构化日志。

 { "cold_start" : false , "function_arn" : "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456790:function:LoggerPowertools" , "function_memory_size" : 128 , "function_name" : "LoggerPowertools" , "function_request_id" : "6eeaa0c9-e58f-45a7-bed2-a4b9d7e65d7e" , "level" : "INFO" , "message" : "Here is some info!" , "service" : "service_undefined" , "timestamp" : "2022-07-19T12:09:28.537Z" , "xray_trace_id" : "1-62d69ef6-dfdbc4be0f59a76c57c52cf8"
}

这将更容易搜索并且不包括无用的字符串化方法。 另外，我们得到了一个Cold_start布尔值。

为了比较，我使用流行且经久不衰的winston库添加了该函数的另一个实现。 让我们看看他们是怎么做的。

功能平均冷启动平均时长代码大小
记录器控制台记录器温斯顿无依赖版本总是最快的。 无论哪种情况，我们都不会增加很多延迟，但 Powertools 更小，因此更快，它为我们提供了cold_start指标。

指标

通常，当谈到指标时，我们会考虑 CPU、延迟和其他运营指标，而 AWS 服务通常会提供开箱即用的这些指标。 当我们最终不得不使用谷歌分析等第三方来推断关键业务事件时，这种想法可能是有缺陷的。 一个更简单的解决方案是让应用程序在业务事件（例如客户注册）发生时发出一个指标。 我们有几个选项可以做到这一点：我们可以使用 aws-sdk，我们可以使用aws-embedded-metrics库，现在我们可以使用 Powertools Metrics。 哪个是最好的？ 让我们来看看。

为此，让我们使用一个不发出指标的函数。

 import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , Context } from ' aws-lambda ' ; export const handler = async ( _event : APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , _context : Context ): Promise < void > => { const workflowSuccess = Math . random () > 0.5 ; if ( workflowSuccess ) { console . log ( ' The workflow was successful! ' ); } else { console . log ( ' The workflow failed. ' ); } };

要了解我们的工作流程是否成功，我们需要查询日志。 啊!

让我们尝试使用来自 aws-sdk-v3 的@aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch库发出指标。

 import { CloudWatchClient , MetricDatum , PutMetricDataCommand , } from ' @aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch ' ; import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , Context } from ' aws-lambda ' ; const client = new CloudWatchClient ({}); export const handler = async ( _event : APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , _context : Context ): Promise < void > => { const workflowSuccess = Math . random () > 0.5 ; let metric : MetricDatum ; if ( workflowSuccess ) { console . log ( ' The workflow was successful! ' ); metric = { MetricName : ' WorkflowSuccess ' , Value : 1 , Unit : ' Count ' }; } else { console . log ( ' The workflow failed. ' ); metric = { MetricName : ' WorkflowFailure ' , Value : 1 , Unit : ' Count ' }; } const command = new PutMetricDataCommand ({ MetricData : [ metric ], Namespace : ' SdkV3Metrics ' , }); await client . send ( command ); };

现在我们在 CloudWatch 中有一些不错的指标！

为此使用 aws-sdk 的缺点是它依赖于 API 调用并且速度有些慢。 我们可以尝试使用 aws-embedded-metrics 来实现相同的目标。 这与 Cloudwatch 自定义指标有何不同？ 更好，社区建设者 Vishnu Prassad 可以告诉你原因。

 import { createMetricsLogger , Unit } from ' aws-embedded-metrics ' ; import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , Context } from ' aws-lambda ' ; export const handler = async ( _event : APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , _context : Context ): Promise < void > => { const workflowSuccess = Math . random () > 0.5 ; const metrics = createMetricsLogger (); metrics . putDimensions ({ Service : ' EMF ' }); if ( workflowSuccess ) { metrics . putMetric ( ' WorkflowSuccess ' , 1 , Unit . Count ); } else { metrics . putMetric ( ' WorkflowFailure ' , 1 , Unit . Count ); } await metrics . flush (); };

除了具有EMF的优势之外，代码更简洁一些。

 import { LambdaInterface } from ' @aws-lambda-powertools/commons ' ; import { Metrics , MetricUnits } from ' @aws-lambda-powertools/metrics ' ; import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , Context } from ' aws-lambda ' ; const metrics = new Metrics ({ namespace : ' Workflow ' }); class Lambda implements LambdaInterface { @ metrics . logMetrics () public async handler ( _event : APIGatewayProxyEventV2 , _context : Context ): Promise < void > { const workflowSuccess = Math . random () > 0.5 ; if ( workflowSuccess ) { metrics . addMetric ( ' WorkflowSuccess ' , MetricUnits . Count , 1 ); } else { metrics . addMetric ( ' WorkflowFailure ' , MetricUnits . Count , 1 ); } } } export const myFunction = new Lambda (); export const handler = myFunction . handler ;

由于需要使用类装饰器，Powertools 版本稍微有点冗长，但仍然不错。 最后我们会更关心性能，所以让我们计算一下这些数字。

功能平均冷启动平均时长代码大小
指标无度量EMF 指标SDKV3 指标电动工具我可能认为这里的 Powertools 实现会包装 aws-embedded-metrics，但显然不是！ 尽管 Powertools 相对于 aws-embedded-metrics 的实际性能优势是微不足道的，但您确实必须欣赏它们如何缩小尺寸。

示踪剂

对于 Tracer 模块，它实际上确实包装了 aws-xray-sdk。 那么为什么我们要使用 Tracer 呢？ 如果 API 更好，并且不会增加太多延迟，那么它可能是值得的。 这是使用 aws-xray-sdk 的示例。 在这种情况下，Lambda 函数正在跟踪一个单独的函数以及一个 SDK 调用（有点无用）获取函数属性。

 import { GetFunctionCommand , GetFunctionCommandOutput , LambdaClient , } from ' @aws-sdk/client-lambda ' ; import { captureAsyncFunc , captureAWSv3Client } from ' aws-xray-sdk-core ' ; import type { Context } from ' aws-lambda ' ; const client = new LambdaClient ({}); captureAWSv3Client ( client ); const getFunction = async ( context : Context ): Promise < GetFunctionCommandOutput > => { const command = new GetFunctionCommand ({ FunctionName : context . functionName , }); return client . send ( command ); }; export const handler = ( _event : unknown , context : Context ): Promise < GetFunctionCommandOutput > => captureAsyncFunc ( ' methodWithCustomTrace ' , async ( subsegment ) => { const fn = await getFunction ( context ); subsegment ?. close (); return fn ; });

那个captureAsyncFunc部分有点尴尬。 对于这样的案例，我真的开始对类装饰器产生好感。 函数装饰器会更好，但如前所述，它们目前在 TypeScript 中不存在。

功能平均冷启动平均时长代码大小
检查性能数据，Powertools 非常轻巧，重量仍然在 7kb 左右，对性能几乎没有影响。 在这种情况下，Powertools 稍微快一些，但我怀疑从长远来看它会花费几毫秒，所以对于 devexp 来说是值得的。

路线图

在决定是否采用 Lambda Powertools 时要考虑的最后一件事是未来可能会提供哪些功能。 更成熟的库Lambda Powertools Python和Lambda Powertools Java包括许多我们可能希望在 Lambda Powertools TypeScript 中看到的有用实用程序。 除了待定的 dotnet 和 golang 库之外，整个Lambda Powertools 路线图并没有告诉我们太多信息，但我们可以深入研究特定于 TypeScript 的问题并瞥见不久的将来。 看起来稳定性仍然是主要优先事项，但有一些有趣的项目，如RFC：最左边一列中的 AWS 数据对象的测试工厂。

社区参与和投票无疑将有助于推动 Powertools 路线图。

结论

对我来说，这简直是天方夜谭。 编写 Lambda 的挑战之一是我们的许多依赖项不是为 Lambda 设计的。 这个库显然是并且团队显然注意以最小的包提供最大的价值。 这些核心实用程序以可访问且易于使用的方式推广最佳实践。 无论您是使用 TypeScript 还是 JavaScript 编写，您都可以享受良好的 IDE 支持和高级 API 来在 Lambda 中实现日志记录、指标和跟踪。

覆盖



原文:

I gave a first look at Lambda Powertools TypeScript back in January of 2022. I was pretty excited for the library at the time, but it came with an admonishment that it wasn't ready for production use. Well, the general availability announcement dropped July 15 so it's time for another look.

Table of Contents

What's Changed

So what's changed in the beta? A glance at the CHANGELOG suggests the answer is that not very much has changed in six months. Lambda Powertools TypeScript still supports class decorators, middy, and a manual API. It still covers the core capabilities of logging, metrics and tracing, and no new capabilities have been added. Outside of some bug fixes and optimization, this is still very much the library I previewed back in January.

ES Modules Support

One change I might've liked to see is support for ES Modules. Interest in ES Modules has been growing quickly in the serverless community, driven largely by the desire to use by Top-Level Await.

Lambda Powertools TypeScript can't be used directly as an ES Modules dependency, but there is an issue open, so please consider adding your +1. For now, it's possible to get by with a require shim or via cjs tricks but it would be great to see native support for ES Modules in Lambda Powertools TypeScript.

Comparisons

Given that I've already gone over these modules in the prior post, I thought I'd instead compare the Lambda Powertools TypeScript modules to similar solutions. I'm looking at the API, the bundled script size, cold start and execution time. To gather metrics, I've written a little app using Step Functions that can run many instances of a function in parallel and capture the metrics.

My benchmarking tool will run each function 50 times aiming to achieve a 20% cold start rate. Code samples are available on GitHub.

Logger

The simplest way to log to CloudWatch from AWS Lambda is with console.log. Doing so adds no bloat to your function, there's no dependency management and writes to CloudWatch are asynchronous, so the operation is non-blocking. Many developers use libraries to guarantee logs are in a structured format and to control the verbosity of logging.

That said, we can use console as a baseline due to its simplicity. If we want a function that simply writes some unstructured logs, we can do something like this.

import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2, APIGatewayProxyResultV2, Context,
} from 'aws-lambda'; export const handler = async ( event: APIGatewayProxyEventV2, context: Context
): Promise<APIGatewayProxyResultV2> => { console.log('event: ', event); console.log('context: ', context); return { statusCode: 200 };
};

Logging out the event yields the stringified context object:

2022-07-19T12:10:45.503Z    2abe532e-2b26-46b5-9a65-884363160556    INFO    context:  {
  callbackWaitsForEmptyEventLoop: [Getter/Setter],
  succeed: [Function (anonymous)],
  fail: [Function (anonymous)],
  done: [Function (anonymous)],
  functionVersion: '$LATEST',
  functionName: 'LoggerConsole',
  memoryLimitInMB: '128',
  logGroupName: '/aws/lambda/LoggerConsole',
  logStreamName: '2022/07/19/[$LATEST]384dbd25ffeb4af49bc22c2ac4f333df',
  clientContext: undefined,
  identity: undefined,
  invokedFunctionArn: 'arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456790:function:LoggerConsole',
  awsRequestId: '2abe532e-2b26-46b5-9a65-884363160556',
  getRemainingTimeInMillis: [Function: getRemainingTimeInMillis]
}

This is pretty noisy and having those succeed, fail and done methods doesn't offer a lot of value.

With Powertools we can inject a more useful context into log messages.

import { Logger } from '@aws-lambda-powertools/logger'; import type { LambdaInterface } from '@aws-lambda-powertools/commons';
import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2, Context } from 'aws-lambda'; const logger = new Logger(); class Lambda implements LambdaInterface { @logger.injectLambdaContext({ logEvent: true }) public async handler( _event: APIGatewayProxyEventV2, _context: Context ): Promise<void> { logger.info('Here is some info!'); }
} export const myFunction = new Lambda();
export const handler = myFunction.handler;

Now we get structured logs.

{ "cold_start": false, "function_arn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456790:function:LoggerPowertools", "function_memory_size": 128, "function_name": "LoggerPowertools", "function_request_id": "6eeaa0c9-e58f-45a7-bed2-a4b9d7e65d7e", "level": "INFO", "message": "Here is some info!", "service": "service_undefined", "timestamp": "2022-07-19T12:09:28.537Z", "xray_trace_id": "1-62d69ef6-dfdbc4be0f59a76c57c52cf8"
}

This is going to be much easier to search and doesn't include useless stringified methods. Plus we get a cold_start boolean tossed in.

For the sake of comparison, I threw in another implementation of the function using the popular and enduring winston library. Let's see how they did.

Function Avg Cold Start Avg Duration Code Size
LoggerConsole 137.24 1.63 771
LoggerPowertools 157.58 1.86 78550
LoggerWinston 184.17 1.62 233142

The no-dependencies version is always going to be the fastest. Powertools adds around 78kb while winston is much heavier at 232kb. In either case we're not adding a lot of latency, but Powertools is smaller and thus faster and it gives us that cold_start metric.

Metrics

Often when it comes to metrics, we think about CPU, latency and other operational metrics and AWS services usually provide those out of the box. This kind of thinking can be flawed when we end up having to use 3rd parties such as google analytics to infer critical business events. A simpler solution is to have the application emit a metric when a business event (say a customer signup) occurs. We have a few options for doing this: We can use aws-sdk, we can use the aws-embedded-metrics lib and now we can use Powertools Metrics. Which is the best? Let's see.

To baseline this, let's use a function that doesn't emit metrics.

import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2, Context } from 'aws-lambda'; export const handler = async ( _event: APIGatewayProxyEventV2, _context: Context
): Promise<void> => { const workflowSuccess = Math.random() > 0.5; if (workflowSuccess) { console.log('The workflow was successful!'); } else { console.log('The workflow failed.'); }
};

To understand whether or not our workflow is successful, we'll need to query logs. Ugh!

Let's try emitting metrics using the @aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch library from aws-sdk-v3.

import { CloudWatchClient, MetricDatum, PutMetricDataCommand,
} from '@aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch'; import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2, Context } from 'aws-lambda'; const client = new CloudWatchClient({}); export const handler = async ( _event: APIGatewayProxyEventV2, _context: Context
): Promise<void> => { const workflowSuccess = Math.random() > 0.5; let metric: MetricDatum; if (workflowSuccess) { console.log('The workflow was successful!'); metric = { MetricName: 'WorkflowSuccess', Value: 1, Unit: 'Count' }; } else { console.log('The workflow failed.'); metric = { MetricName: 'WorkflowFailure', Value: 1, Unit: 'Count' }; } const command = new PutMetricDataCommand({ MetricData: [metric], Namespace: 'SdkV3Metrics', }); await client.send(command);
};

Now we have some nice metrics in CloudWatch!

The downside of using aws-sdk for this is that it relies on API calls and is somewhat slow. We can try to achieve the same thing using aws-embedded-metrics. How is this different from Cloudwatch custom metrics? It's better and fellow Community Builder Vishnu Prassad can tell you why.

import { createMetricsLogger, Unit } from 'aws-embedded-metrics'; import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2, Context } from 'aws-lambda'; export const handler = async ( _event: APIGatewayProxyEventV2, _context: Context
): Promise<void> => { const workflowSuccess = Math.random() > 0.5; const metrics = createMetricsLogger(); metrics.putDimensions({ Service: 'EMF' }); if (workflowSuccess) { metrics.putMetric('WorkflowSuccess', 1, Unit.Count); } else { metrics.putMetric('WorkflowFailure', 1, Unit.Count); } await metrics.flush();
};

In addition to having the advantage of EMF, the code is a bit more concise.

import { LambdaInterface } from '@aws-lambda-powertools/commons';
import { Metrics, MetricUnits } from '@aws-lambda-powertools/metrics'; import type { APIGatewayProxyEventV2, Context } from 'aws-lambda'; const metrics = new Metrics({ namespace: 'Workflow' }); class Lambda implements LambdaInterface { @metrics.logMetrics() public async handler( _event: APIGatewayProxyEventV2, _context: Context ): Promise<void> { const workflowSuccess = Math.random() > 0.5; if (workflowSuccess) { metrics.addMetric('WorkflowSuccess', MetricUnits.Count, 1); } else { metrics.addMetric('WorkflowFailure', MetricUnits.Count, 1); } }
} export const myFunction = new Lambda();
export const handler = myFunction.handler;

The Powertools version is just a tad more verbose due to the need to use class decorators, but still not bad. In the end we'll care more about performance, so let's run those numbers.

Function Avg Cold Start Avg Duration Code Size
MetricsNone 135.25 0.93 826
MetricsEMF 146.75 1.33 29456
MetricsSDKV3 225.37 32 257026
MetricsPowertools 141.3 1.33 7942

I might've thought the Powertools implementation here would wrap aws-embedded-metrics, but obviously not! Although the actual performance benefit of Powertools over aws-embedded-metrics is marginal, you really have to appreciate how they've kept the size down.

Tracer

In the case of the Tracer module, it actually does wrap the aws-xray-sdk. So why would we use Tracer instead? If the API is nicer and it's not adding much in the way of latency, it could be worth it. Here's an example of using aws-xray-sdk. In this case the Lambda Function is tracing a separate function as well as an SDK call to (somewhat uselessly) get function properties.

import { GetFunctionCommand, GetFunctionCommandOutput, LambdaClient,
} from '@aws-sdk/client-lambda';
import { captureAsyncFunc, captureAWSv3Client } from 'aws-xray-sdk-core'; import type { Context } from 'aws-lambda';
const client = new LambdaClient({}); captureAWSv3Client(client); const getFunction = async ( context: Context
): Promise<GetFunctionCommandOutput> => { const command = new GetFunctionCommand({ FunctionName: context.functionName, }); return client.send(command);
}; export const handler = ( _event: unknown, context: Context
): Promise<GetFunctionCommandOutput> => captureAsyncFunc('methodWithCustomTrace', async (subsegment) => { const fn = await getFunction(context); subsegment?.close(); return fn; });

That captureAsyncFunc part is a bit awkward. How does Powertools do this?

import { LambdaInterface } from '@aws-lambda-powertools/commons';
import { Tracer } from '@aws-lambda-powertools/tracer';
import { GetFunctionCommand, GetFunctionCommandOutput, LambdaClient,
} from '@aws-sdk/client-lambda'; import type { Context } from 'aws-lambda'; const client = new LambdaClient({}); const tracer = new Tracer({ serviceName: 'powertoolsTracer' });
tracer.captureAWSv3Client(client); class Lambda implements LambdaInterface { @tracer.captureMethod() public async methodWithCustomTrace( context: Context ): Promise<GetFunctionCommandOutput> { const command = new GetFunctionCommand({ FunctionName: context.functionName, }); return client.send(command); } @tracer.captureLambdaHandler() public async handler( _event: unknown, context: Context ): Promise<GetFunctionCommandOutput> { return this.methodWithCustomTrace(context); }
} export const handlerClass = new Lambda();
export const handler = handlerClass.handler;

I'm really starting to warm to class decorators for cases like this. Function decorators would be better, but as previously discussed, they don't currently exist in TypeScript.

Function Avg Cold Start Avg Duration Code Size
TracerXRay 266.91 50.56 410514
TracerPowertools 265.42 48.25 417980

Checking the performance numbers, Powertools is extremely light, still weighing in at around 7kb and barely impacting performance at all. In this case, Powertools is slightly faster, but I suspect over the long run it's costing a couple of ms, so worth it for the devexp.

Roadmap

One final thing to consider when deciding whether to adopt Lambda Powertools is what features may be in store in the future. The more mature libraries Lambda Powertools Python and Lambda Powertools Java include a number of useful utilities that we may like to see in Lambda Powertools TypeScript. The overall Lambda Powertools Roadmap doesn't tell us very much other than pending dotnet and golang libraries, but we can drill down into TypeScript-specific issues and glimpse the immediate future. It looks like stability is still the main priority, but there are some interesting items like RFC: Testing Factories for AWS Data Objects in the leftmost column.

Community engagement and voting will no doubt help to drive the Powertools roadmap.

Conclusion

To me, this is a no-brainer. One of the challenges in writing Lambda is that many of our dependencies were not designed for Lambda. This library obviously was and the team obviously took care to deliver maximum value in a minimal package. These core utilities promote best practices in a way that is accessible and easy to use. Whether you're writing in TypeScript or JavaScript, you can enjoy good IDE support and a high-level API to implement logging, metrics and tracing in Lambda.

COVER

Items.Title: 宝藏，你远望的成功，就在你自己里面。 | The treasure, the success you are looking far away is inside yourself.
Items.Description:
从前，有一个人继承了很多财富。 他不知道这笔财富的真正价值，因为他很容易就得到了这份财富，没有任何艰辛和挣扎。 因此，他浪费了所有的财富，变得贫穷。 一切都消失了，钱、房子、家具； 只剩下他一个人了。

当他一无所有时，他想起了上帝并转向他：“主啊！ 你给了我财富，财富却没有了。 你给了我房子，房子没了。 所以我向你寻求庇护。 我求你给我财富”。 他回到上帝面前呼求祂。 正如先知所说：“信徒就像长笛。 它在空的时候产生音乐”。 听众啊！ 让自己保持空虚，在两根手指的陪伴下保持快乐。 泪水从他的眼眶中滑落，他——追求财富的人——不求挣扎，只求财富。

当他变得谦卑时，怜悯之门就向他敞开了。 有没有人敲他的门却无人回答？ 于是谦卑的人做了一个梦。 在梦中，他听到天上有声音对他说：“你的愿望将在埃及实现。 去那里，因为你的祈祷会在那里被接受。 在埃及某处某处埋藏着宝藏。 你应该毫不拖延地去那里寻找它”

于是他从巴格达一路跋涉到埃及。 当他到达那里时，埃及的景象使他充满希望。 他想起了埋葬在特定地点的珍贵宝物。

但经过这漫长的旅程，他的口袋里已经没有一分钱了，没有花销，连面包都没有。 他想过向人乞讨，但羞耻和荣誉阻止了他。 他的“自我”因饥饿而摇摆不定。 除了乞求，他别无他法。 他想过在夜深人静的时候乞讨，免得觉得丢脸。 他以为我会在夜里大喊大叫，以至于有人从屋顶掉下一分钱。 想到这里，他在街上闲逛。 有时羞耻和荣誉会冻住他的脚，有时饥饿会让他走路。 前进一步，后退一步； 我应该乞求还是应该用干燥的嘴唇睡觉？ 三分之一的夜晚在同样的想法中过去了。

突然，夜间巡逻队抓住了他，开始用他的警棍无情地殴打他。 事实上，那些日子里，人们遭受了许多盗贼的折磨。 以至于国王下令警察砍掉夜行者的手，即使他是国王的亲戚。 所以警察在国王的压力下很大。 夜巡在此人头上和后背上打了无数下。 乞丐哭着求饶：“不要打人，我会告诉你真相”。 夜巡停了下来，对他说：“好，我给你一个机会。 你不属于这里，你是外国人，所以告诉我你的意图是什么？ 官员们责骂警察盗贼多。 现在告诉我你的伙伴在哪里； 否则，你会为他们的所作所为受到报复。”

他对夜间巡逻队说：“我不是小偷，也不是罪犯，我是埃及的陌生人，我属于巴格达”。 然后他讲述了他梦见埋在埃及的宝藏的整个故事。 夜间巡逻队在他的故事中闻到了真相，这让他的心融化了。 因为心被真言安慰，就像口渴的人被水安慰一样。 夜巡的眼里掉下了泪水； 不是因为言语枯燥，而是因为一颗真心的芬芳。

夜巡对他说：“你既不是小偷，也不是罪犯。 你是一个好人，但一个非常愚蠢的人，因为一个梦想而长途跋涉。 你的智慧中没有一丝光亮。 你走这么远的路，只是因为你的愚蠢和贪婪。 我曾多次看到这样的梦：‘宝藏被埋在巴格达，在某某街道上的某某房子里。 去那里寻找它。 而你，就在一个梦想之后，毫不犹豫地走了这么远的路吗？”

毫无疑问，傻瓜的梦想是根据他的智慧——和他一样一文不值。 做梦的人对自己说：“宝藏在我自己的家里。 那为什么要抱怨那个地方呢？ 我——坐在宝藏上——因乞讨而死，因为我粗心和盲目。” 他被这个好消息陶醉了。 他的痛苦已经不复存在了。 他赞美真主一千次。 走！ 因为我今天尝到了美味的食物。 我对自己贫穷的错觉视而不见。 不管你说我是傻还是聪明，我已经找到了我内心所寻找的东西。 哦，尊敬的先生！ 你可以叫我病人。 我可能对你有耐心，但我认为自己很健康。 如果案子倒过来，那就更悲哀了。 如果我对你来说是一朵花，但我认为自己是一根刺。 当一个基地对他说“这里没有人认识你”时，苦行僧回答说：“没有人认识我也没关系，你难道没有听说过苦行僧吗？ 至少我知道自己，我是谁”。

这个人在上帝面前鞠躬叩头，从埃及返回巴格达。 一路上，他都被全能的真主的征兆和预兆所震撼，它们是如何以一种任何心灵都无法感知的方式表现出来的。 他对自己的财富和追求的道路是如何相反感到困惑。 他对自己说：“他在哪里使人充满希望，从哪里倾泻财富！ 这是什么智慧让我离家出走，走错了路？ 我什至加快了错误的道路，以至于每时每刻，我都在远离我所寻求的。 然后他使同样的错误道路成为我引导正确道路的工具。”

祂把迷途的路变成信心的路，使公义的路通向迷途。 因此，没有一个义人是没有恐惧的，没有一个叛徒是没有希望的。 他将解药藏在毒药中，以便他们可以对他说：“隐藏的恩典之主”。

那人回到了自己的家中，发现了宝藏。 靠着神的恩典，他的祈祷被接受了。 这样你就可以观察到“那位”的智慧，他是最聪明的。

最重要的是，Maulana Rumi 从这个故事中获得了一些深刻的精神意义，否则人们永远无法理解。 然而，这个故事最受欢迎的版本是保罗·科埃略的畅销书《炼金术士》。 巴西人 Paulo Coelho 首先阅读了这个故事的版本，该版本由阿根廷故事作家豪尔赫·路易斯·博尔赫斯 (Jorge Luis Borges) 撰写（来源：《纽约客》）。

无论如何，我在这里写的故事取自伟大的波斯诗人毛拉娜·贾拉鲁丁·鲁米的《马斯纳维》第 6 卷。



原文:

Once, there was a man who received a lot of wealth in inheritance. He did not know the true value of this wealth because he got this wealth easily without any hardship and struggle. Thus, he wasted all his wealth and became poor. Everything was gone, the money, the houses, the furniture; and he was left alone.

And when he was left with nothing, he remembered God and turned to Him: “O Lord! You gave me wealth and the wealth is gone. You gave me houses and houses are gone. So I ask refuge from You. And I ask You to give me wealth”. And he returned to God and called upon Him. As the Prophet said, “Believer is like a flute. It produces music when it is empty”. O listener! Keep yourself empty and remain joyful in the company of the two fingers. Tears dropped from his eyes and he — the seeker of wealth — asked for wealth without asking for struggle.

When he became humble, the doors of mercy opened upon him. Is there anyone who knocks on His door and is not answered? So the humble man saw a dream. In his dream, he heard a voice from heaven saying to him, “ Your desire will be fulfilled in Egypt. Go there as your prayer will be accepted there. There is a treasure buried in a such-a-such a spot in Egypt. You should go there in search of it without any delay”

So he traveled all the way from Baghdad to Egypt. And when he reached there, the sight of Egypt raised his hopes. He remembered the precious treasure that is buried at the specific spot.

But after this long journey, he had no single penny left in his pocket, not for expenses, not even for bread. He thought about begging from people but shame and honor refrained him. His “self” swerved with hunger. He had no other way but to beg. He thought about begging in the darkness of night so that he doesn’t feel ashamed. He thought I will bawl in the night so that someone drops a penny from the roof. Thinking this, he wandered in the streets. Sometimes shame and honor froze his feet and sometimes hunger made him walk. One step forward, one step backward; Should I beg or should I sleep with the dry lips? One-third of the night passed in thinking the same.

And suddenly, the night patrol seized him and started beating him mercilessly with his nightstick. As it occurred, the people had suffered much from the night thieves those days. Such that the king had ordered police to cut the hand of he who strolls in the night, even if he is the king’s relative. So the police were much under the pressure from the king. The night patrol beat the person with countless strokes on his head and his back. The beggar cried for mercy, “Don’t strike so that I may tell you the truth”. The night patrol stopped and said to him, “Okay, I give you a chance. You don’t belong here and you are a foreigner so tell me what are your intentions? The officials scold the police for the abundance of thieves. Now tell me where are your partners; otherwise, you will be revenged for the doings of them all.”

He said to the night patrol, “I am not a thief nor a criminal, I am a stranger in Egypt and I belong to Baghdad”. And then he narrated the whole story about his dream of the treasure buried in Egypt. The night patrol smelled truth in his story and it melted his heart. It is because the heart is comforted by true words just as a thirsty man is comforted by water. The eyes of the night patrol dropped tears; not because of dry speech but due to the fragrance of a true heart.

The night patrol said to him, “You are neither a thief nor a criminal. You are a good man but a very foolish person as you traveled this long distance just because of a dream. There isn’t the least spark of light in your intelligence. You traveled this long distance only because of your foolishness and greed. I have seen a dream multiple times that: ‘A treasure is buried in Baghdad, in a such-a-such house on the such-a-such street. Go there and search for it.’ ” The night patrol told the name of the same person and it was the same house and the street where he lived. “I have seen this multiple times in dreams that there is a treasure buried at a specific place in Baghdad but I never left my home on account of this. And you have traveled this long distance just after a dream without any hesitation?”

No doubt, the dream of the foolish is according to his intelligence — it is worthless just like him. The person who dreamed said to himself, “The treasure is in my own house. Then why complain about that place? I — sitting over the treasure — am dead with beggary because I am heedless and blind.” He became intoxicated by this good news. His pain was no more. He praised Allah, a thousand times. “My delicious food depended upon the taste of this hardship. Walk! As I have tasted a wonderful food today. I was blind to the delusion that I am poor. No matter whether you call me foolish or intelligent, I have found what my heart searched for. O, Respected Sir! You can call me a patient. I may be patient with you but I consider myself to be healthy. It would have been much more sorrowful if the case was reversed. If I was a flower to you but I considered myself a thorn. Haven’t you heard of the dervish when a base said to him, ‘No-one knows you here’ and the dervish replied, ‘It doesn’t matter if no-one knows me? At least I know myself, who I am ”.

Bowing and prostrating in front of God, the person returned from Egypt to Baghdad. All the way, he was wonder-struck by the signs and omens of Allah Almighty, how they are manifested in a way that no mind can perceive. He was bewildered at how his wealth and his path of seeking were just the opposite. He was saying to himself, “Of where did He make hopeful, and from where did He shower wealth! What wisdom was this that He caused me to go forth from home and take the wrong path? I even hastened on the wrong path so that every moment, I was moving away from what I sought. And then he made the same wrong path an instrument of my guide to the right path.”

He turns a stray path into the road of faith and makes the road of righteousness lead to stray paths. So that no righteous man is without fear and no traitor is without hope. He has hidden the antidote inside the poison so that they may say to Him, “The Lord of Hidden Grace”.

The person returned to his home and discovered the treasure. By the Grace of God, his prayer was accepted. So that you may observe the wisdom of “The One” who’s wise of all.

Maulana Rumi has described the story with more details and much more explanation of the meaning. Above all, Maulana Rumi has derived some deep spiritual meanings from the story which otherwise someone could never understand. However, the most popular version of this story is Paulo Coelho’s bestseller “The Alchemist”. Paulo Coelho — who is Brazilian — had first read the version of this story which was written by Jorge Luis Borges, an Argentine story-writer (Source: The New Yorker).

Anyway, the story that I’ve written here is taken from Volume 6 of “Masnavi” by the great Persian poet Maulana Jalaluddin Rumi.

Items.Title: Git 基础知识：更改您的上一条提交消息 | Git basics: Changing your last commit message
Items.Description:
在编写提交消息时，您可能会不小心打错字。
或者因为您快速尝试解决错误，您不小心留下了旧的提交消息并推送了它。

保持消息有意义是一种很好的做法，这样您就会知道在哪个提交中更改了什么。

我在我的 GitHub 存储库上提交了错误的提交消息，以展示它是如何工作的。

在上图中，我们看到我通过消息提交了一些更改：“修复：图像名称错误消息”。

让我们看看如何解决这个问题。

修复非推送的提交消息

如果您尚未推送代码，则更容易修复。

您可以运行以下命令。

这将打开一个 vim 编辑器，您可以在其中更改提交的消息。

要编辑文本，请按i键，要停止，请按esc然后wq保存文件。

但是，更快的方法是使用-m属性，该属性可用于修改提交消息。

 git commit --amend -m "fix: image name correct message."

我们可以在没有推送新提交的情况下看到提交消息的更改。

修复推送的提交消息

但是，例如，如果我们已经将错误消息推送到 GitHub，会发生什么情况。

不用担心，我们仍然可以修复它而不会搞砸。

如果我们正在处理最后一次提交，我们可以再次运行以下命令：

 git commit --amend -m "fix: image name"

下一步是在覆盖之前的提交消息的同时进行推送。 为此，请使用以下命令：



原文:

You might accidentally make a typo while writing your commit message.
Or because you quickly tried to solve a bug, you accidentally left the old commit message and pushed that.

It's a good practice to keep the message meaningful, so you'll know what you change in which commit.

I made a wrong commit message on my GitHub repo to showcase how it works.

In the image above, we see I committed some changes with the message: "fix: image name wrong message".

Let's see how we can fix that.

Fixing a non-pushed commit message

If you haven't pushed your code yet, it's easier to fix.

You can run the following command.

This will open up a vim editor where you can change the commit's message.

To edit the text, press the i key, and to stop, press esc then wq to save the file.

However, a faster way is to use the -m property, which can be used to amend the commit message.

git commit --amend -m "fix: image name correct message."

We can see the commit message altered without pushing a new commit.

Fixing a pushed commit message

However, what happens if we already pushed the wrong message to GitHub, for instance.

No worries, we can still fix it without messing things up.

If we are addressing the last commit, we can again run the following command:

git commit --amend -m "fix: image name"

Items.Title: 5 个可用于在 React 应用中拖放的 NPM 包🙌 | 5 NPM Packages you can use for Drag & Drop in your React app 🙌
Items.Description:
如果您正在构建像 Canva 这样的图形设计工具，拖放功能可以增强您的用户体验。 几天前，我开发了一个名为Cover-Builder的博客文章封面设计工具，可以让您为博客文章创建漂亮的封面图片。 该工具严重依赖拖放功能。 这是第一次，我希望将拖放添加到我的任何应用程序中，幸运的是找到了一些很酷的包。

注意：您还需要安装第二个包react-dnd-html5-backend以便 react-dnd 可以在后台使用 HTML 5 拖放 API。

它为您提供多拖、自动滚动以及对鼠标、键盘和触摸设备的支持等功能。

它为您提供了一个可拖动的组件，您可以将其包裹在要拖动的元素周围，仅此而已。 在这里，您还可以传递默认位置、轴和其他一些属性以进行自定义。

与 React-Draggable 一样，它还允许您传递一些道具以进行更好的自定义。
注意：它还有一个绑定道具，允许您在特定区域绑定拖放。

该软件包使您可以轻松地进行文件拖放处理。

如果您使用过其他软件包，请在评论中告诉我。

感谢您的阅读。



原文:

Drag & Drop is something that can enhance your user's experience if you are building graphic designing tools like Canva. A few days back, I developed a blog post cover designing tool titled Cover-Builder that allows you to create nice cover images for your blog posts. The tool heavily relies on Drag & Drop feature. This was the first time, I was looking to add Drag & Drop into any of my apps & luckily found some cool packages.

1. React-Dnd - When it comes to dragging into different parts of your application then this is the most used package by developers.
Note: You will also need to install a second package react-dnd-html5-backend so that react-dnd can use HTML 5 drag-drop API under the hood.

2. React-Beautiful-Dnd - Most famous package to create beautiful drag and drop lists that anyone can use - even people who cannot see. It provides you features like multi-dragging, auto-scrolling, and support for mouse, keyboard, and touch devices.

3. React-Draggable - Unlike the react-dnd package, you need to install only a single package here. It provides you a draggable component that you can wrap around the element you are looking to drag & that's all. Here you can also pass props like defaultposition, axis & some others for customization.

4. React-Rnd - If you are looking to resize your items with Drag &Drop then this is for you. Like React-Draggable it also allows you to pass some props for better customization.
Note: It also has a bound prop which allows you to bound Drag & Drops in a particular region.

5. React-Dropzone - Creating Drag & Drop feature for uploading files is not so easy task, many JS events are involved in it. This package makes file Drag & Drop handling easy for you.

🏁 The aim of the article is to give you a brief look of available Drag & Drop packages which can make Drag & Drop easy for you. If you have used another package let me in know in the comments.

Thank you for reading.

ອີກຄວາມພິເສດໜຶ່ງຂອງ NFT ກໍ່ຄື: ​ເຮົາສາມາດກວດສອບໄດ້ວ່າ NFT ດັ່ງກ່າວມີການຊື້ຂາຍແລກປ່ຽນກັນມາທີ່ລາຄາເທົ່າໃດ ແລະ ຜູ້ທີ່ສ້າງສັນຜົນງານ NFT ດັ່ງກ່າວກໍ່ຈະສາມາດກຳນົດຄ່າທຳນຽມໃນການຊື້ຂາຍຜົນງານຂອງເຮົາໄດ້ອີກ, ໝາຍຄວາມວ່າທຸກໆຄັ້ງທີ່ມີການຊື້ຂາຍຜົນງານຂອງເຮົາ ເຮົາກໍ່ຈະໄດ້ຄ່າທຳນຽມຈາກການຊື້ຂາຍທຸກຄັ້ງນັ້ນເອງ. ອີກຄວາມພິເສດໜຶ່ງຂອງ NFT ກໍ່ຄື: ​ເຮົາສາມາດກວດສອບໄດ້ວ່າ NFT ດັ່ງກ່າວມີການຊື້ຂາຍແລກປ່ຽນກັນມາທີ່ລາຄາເທົ່າໃດ ແລະ ຜູ້ທີ່ສ້າງສັນຜົນງານ NFT ດັ່ງກ່າວກໍ່ຈະສາມາດກຳນົດຄ່າທຳນຽມໃນການຊື້ຂາຍຜົນງານຂອງເຮົາໄດ້ອີກ, ໝາຍຄວາມວ່າທຸກໆຄັ້ງທີ່ມີການຊື້ຂາຍຜົນງານຂອງເຮົາ ເຮົາກໍ່ຈະໄດ້ຄ່າທຳນຽມຈາກການຊື້ຂາຍທຸກຄັ້ງນັ້ນເອງ.

原文:

ໃນຊ່ວງປີ 2012 ຖືວ່າເປັນຍຸກທີ່ຄົນເລີ່ມໃຫ້ຄວາມສົນໃຈໃນ Bitcoin ແລະ ໃນຕອນນັ້ນມັນກໍ່ມີແນວຄິດໜຶ່ງເກີດຂຶ້ນມານັ້ນກໍ່ຄື: Colored Coins. ໂດຍປົກກະຕິການເຮັດທຸລະກຳຂອງ Bitcoin ເຮົາຈະໃສ່ຄ່າຫຍັງກໍ່ໄດ້ເພື່ອຈະບອກວ່າການເຮັດທຸລະກຳນັ້ນໆກ່ຽວກັບຫຍັງ, ເຊິ່ງແນວຄິດຂອງ Colored Coins ກໍ່ຈະແທນຄ່າການເຮັດທຸລະກຳຕ່າງໆຂອງ Bitcoin ໃຫ້ມີຄວາມໝາຍຕາມໂລກຂອງຄວາມເປັນຈິງເຊັ່ນ: ຄູປອງ, ກຳມະສິດທີ່ດິນ ຫຼື ກຳມະສິດໃດໆກໍ່ຕາມທີ່ເຮົາຕ້ອງການປ່ຽນຊັບສິນເຫຼົ່ານັ້ນໃຫ້ມາຢູ່ໃນຮູບແບບ Digital Asset. ຖ້າເຮົາໃສ່ຄ່າຕ່າງໆເຫຼົ່ານັ້ນເຂົ້າໃນທຸລະກຳຂອງ Bitcoin ຂໍ້ມູນເຫຼົ່ານັ້ນກໍ່ຈະໄປຢູ່ເທິງ Blockchain ຂອງ Bitcoin ເຊິ່ງເຮົາສາມາດກວດສອບໄດ້ພາຍຫຼັງວ່າໃຜເປັນຜູ້ຄອບຄອງຊັບສິນເຫຼົ່ານັ້ນຢູ່. ຈາກ Concept ທີ່ກ່າວມາຖືວ່າເປັນຮາກຖານທີ່ສຳຄັນທີ່ເຮັດໃຫ້ເກີດມີ NFT ໃນທຸກມື້ນີ້.

ເຂົ້າໃຈ NFT ໃນ 30 ວິນາທີ

ຈາກຍຸກຂອງ Bitcoin ເຮົາກໍ່ກ້າວເຂົ້າມາສູ່ຍຸກຂອງ Ethereum ເຊິ່ງຖືວ່າເປັນເຄືອຂ່າຍ Blockchain ທີ່ເຮົາສາມາດຂຽນ smart contact ເຂົ້າໄປໄດ້, ເຊິ່ງຖ້າເຮົາຢາກຈະມີ Token ຫຼື ສະກຸນເງິນເປັນຂອງຕົວເອງ ເຮົາກໍ່ສາມາດຂຽນ smart contact ຂອງເຮົາເອງໄດ້ເລີຍ ແລະ NFT ເອງກໍ່ເປັນ Token ຊະນິດໜຶ່ງທີ່ບໍ່ສາມາດທົດແທນກັນໄດ້.

ຄວາມແຕກຕ່າງລະຫວ່າງ Token ແບບປົກກະຕິ ແລະ NFT Token

ຕົວຢ່າງ: ເຮົາມີ Token ໜຶ່ງທີ່ຊື່ວ່າ KIP Token ເຊິ່ງມີ Max Supply ຢູ່ທີ່ 10 ລ້ານຫຼຽນ, ເຊິ່ງທຸກຄົນທີ່ຖື Token ນີ້ຢູ່ກໍ່ຈະສາມາດແລກປ່ຽນຫຼຽນນີ້ກັນໄປມາໄດ້ເພາະວ່າ 1 KIP = 1 KIP ໝາຍຄວາມວ່າ 1 KIP ຂອງແຕ່ລະຄົນມີຄ່າເທົ່າກັນ ແລະ ສາມາດທົດແທນກັນໄດ້ນັ້ນເອງ. ຖ້າເຮົາມີ NFT Token ທີ່ມີ Max Supply ຢູ່ພຽງແຕ່ 1 ຫຼຽນ(1 Token) ແລະ ຖ້າເຮົາຕ້ອງການທີ່ຈະຄອບຄອງຫຼຽນດັ່ງກ່າວເຮົາກໍ່ອາດຈະຕ້ອງໃຊ້ 10 KIP ເພື່ອແລກມາ, ທັງນີ້ກໍ່ຂຶ້ນກັບຄວາມເພິ່ງພໍໃຈຂອງຜູ້ຖືຄອງກ່ອນໜ້ານີ້ວ່າຈະໃຊ້ເທົ່າໃດຫຼຽນໃນການແລກ.

ອີກຄວາມພິເສດໜຶ່ງຂອງ NFT ກໍ່ຄື: ​ເຮົາສາມາດກວດສອບໄດ້ວ່າ NFT ດັ່ງກ່າວມີການຊື້ຂາຍແລກປ່ຽນກັນມາທີ່ລາຄາເທົ່າໃດ ແລະ ຜູ້ທີ່ສ້າງສັນຜົນງານ NFT ດັ່ງກ່າວກໍ່ຈະສາມາດກຳນົດຄ່າທຳນຽມໃນການຊື້ຂາຍຜົນງານຂອງເຮົາໄດ້ອີກ, ໝາຍຄວາມວ່າທຸກໆຄັ້ງທີ່ມີການຊື້ຂາຍຜົນງານຂອງເຮົາ ເຮົາກໍ່ຈະໄດ້ຄ່າທຳນຽມຈາກການຊື້ຂາຍທຸກຄັ້ງນັ້ນເອງ.

ແລ້ວ NFT ມີມູນຄ່າໄດ້ແນວໃດ?

ໂດຍປົກກະຕິແລ້ວ NFT ຈະຖືກແທນຄ່າດ້ວຍຮູບພາບຜົນງານສີລະປະ, ເພງ, ວີດີໂອ, ໄອເທັມຕ່າງໆໃນເກມ ແລະ ຍັງມີອີກຫຼາຍສິ່ງທີຖືກນຳມາສ້າງເປັນ NFT, ເມື່ອມັນຖືກແທນຄ່າດ້ວຍສິ່ງຕ່າງໆເຫຼົ່ານັ້ນ ມັນກໍ່ປຽບເໝືອນ Rare item ໃນໂລກຂອງ Blockchain ມັນຈຶ່ງເຮັດໃຫ້ຄົນທີ່ສົນໃຈຜົນງານເຫຼົ່ານັ້ນຊື້ເກັບສະສົມ ຫຼື ອາດຈະຊື້ເພື່ອເກັງກຳໄລ.

ເຮົາຈະສ້າງຜົນງານ NFT ຂຶ້ນມາໄດ້ແນວໃດ?

ໂດຍປົກກະຕິແລ້ວເຮົາສາມາດສ້າງ NFT ຂຶ້ນມາໄດ້ 2 ວິທີຄື:

  • ຂຽນ Smart Contact ເອງເພື່ອສ້າງ Token ຂອງເຮົາຂຶ້ນມາແລ້ວແທນຄ່າ Token ເຫຼົ່ານັ້ນດ້ວຍຮູບພາບ ຫຼື ຜົນງານຮູບແບບອື່ນໆ, ຫຼັງຈາກນັ້ນກໍ່ໃຫ້ຄົນເຂົ້າໄປ mint ຜົນງານຂອງເຮົາຈາກ website ທີ່ເຮົາຂຽນ.

  • ອັບໂຫຼດຜົນງານຂອງເຮົາຂຶ້ນໄປເທິງ Platform ຕ່າງໆ, ເຊິ່ງຈະເປັນການໃຊ້ smart contact ຂອງ platform ເຫຼົ່ານັ້ນສ້າງ Token ໃຫ້ເຮົາແທນ.

ທັງ 2 ວິທີນີ້ກໍ່ຕ້ອງໃຊ້ຄ່າ gas ເຊັ່ນກັນ.

ບົດຄວາມຕໍ່ໄປຈະມາອະທິບາຍເລື່ອງ NFT ສະບັບ Developer ແບບງົງໆ, ຝາກຕິດຕາມກັນຄືກັບເຈົ້າກຳນາຍເວນແນ່ເດີ😘

In the words do not pay pay calculation? But the calculation here is in pixels. It works across the entire screen in all types of applications. You can measure anything. It will come in handy for those who need everything pixel perfect. getpixelsnap.com

I found this website very useful. Here the description of various parts of UI is given in very organized and detailed manner. uiplaybook.dev

If you want to make a resume very simply in a short time, then you can definitely visit this website. Here are various templates and examples from which you can create something unique yourself with an idea. standardresume.co

原文:

Bạn đang có nhu cầu mua biển kim loại ăn mòn nhưng còn băn khoăn về chất liệu sản phẩm này không biết có tốt không?

Bạn đang lo lắng về giá thành của mẫu biển làm bảng hiệu quảng cáo inox ăn mòn?

Bạn muốn chọn cho mình 1 địa chỉ uy tín, mang đến cho bạn những mẫu biển hiệu chất lượng nhưng vẫn đảm bảo có được giá bán cạnh tranh?

Nếu đây là điều mà bạn đang lo lắng và cần tìm hiểu khi có nhu cầu mua biển ăn mòn thì đừng bỏ qua những thông tin được nhắc đến dưới đây nhé. Chúng tôi sẽ cùng bạn tìm hiểu một số nội dung cơ bản về sản phẩm để việc mua hàng được thuận lợi hơn, cùng tham khảo ngay nhé.

Đánh giá về chất lượng của biển ăn mòn kim loại hiện nay

Một trong những dòng biển quảng cáo nổi bật nhất trên thị trường hiện nay phải kể đến chính là dòng biển inox ăn mòn và biển đồng ăn mòn. Sản phẩm được chế tác nhờ phương pháp ăn mòn bề mặt kim loại bằng những hóa chất chuyên dụng, từ đó có được nội dung của biển cực kỳ tinh tế, sắc nét; và mang đến cho người dùng những sản phẩm có được độ hoàn thiện cao hơn.

Các đơn vị gia công biển ăn mòn thường lựa chọn sử dụng những vật liệu inox hay đồng kim loại tốt nhất, hàng chuẩn loại 1, sử dụng công nghệ và máy móc hiện đại trong khâu chế tác. Vì thế mà mỗi sản phẩm làm ra đều đạt được tính thẩm mỹ cao, tuổi thọ bề lâu cùng với khả năng chịu nhiệt cao, chịu được nhiều tác động của môi trường bên ngoài. Sản phẩm là một sự lựa chọn tối ưu về chi phí cho khách hàng, các đơn vị, công ty.

Hình thức hiện đại của một mẫu biển ăn mòn

Hiện nay những tấm biển ăn mòn này đang được ứng dụng một cách cực kỳ rộng rãi, chúng được lựa chọn để làm các mẫu biển báo công trình, biển chỉ dẫn – sơ đồ tòa nhà, biển tên phòng ban hay các khu vực làm việc, biển tên công ty, biển tên chức danh, biển số nhà, biển nội quy, biển cẩm, logo công ty và các loại tem nhãn khác… Tùy theo nhu cầu và mục đích sử dụng khác nhau để người dùng đưa ra cho mình những sự lựa chọn thích hợp nhất.

Tư vấn địa chỉ làm biển ăn mòn hàng đẹp, giá rẻ

Khi có nhu cầu về làm biển inox ăn mòn hay đồng ăn mòn thì quý khách cần phải tìm cho mình những xưởng gia công uy tín, chuyên nghiệp để có được những sản phẩm chất lượng hàng đầu cho mình. Bạn đang có nhu cầu tìm cho mình những mẫu biển inox ăn mòn hay biển đồng ăn mòn và cần tìm cho mình một đơn vị bán hàng uy tín thì có thể đến ngay với công ty mica Sơn Nam chúng tôi nhé.

Tất cả những sản phẩm biển ăn mòn được bán tại xưởng chúng tôi đều được gia công kỹ càng và tỉ mỉ trên công nghệ hiện đại với máy móc tân tiến nhất. Vì thế mà việc chế tác luôn đạt được độ chính xác cao, kể cả những mẫu chữ nhỏ hay những đường bo gấp khúc cũng đều được thực hiện nhanh gọn nhất. Vì thế mà sản phẩm biển inox ăn mòn được làm ra từ xưởng đều có độ tinh xảo, sắc nét đầy ấn tượng.

Chọn mua biển công ty ăn mòn đẹp tại Hà Nội

Đơn vị chúng tôi chuyên thi công làm bảng hiệu ăn mòn cao cấp, giá rẻ đáp ứng mọi yêu cầu mà các khách hàng đặt ra. Đến đây bạn sẽ có được cho mình những tư vấn chi tiết nhất về từng mặt hàng cùng sự phục vụ tận tình từ đội ngũ nhân viên, đảm bảo sẽ đem đến cho bạn những sản phẩm ấn tượng, hoàn thiện nhất. Công ty mica Sơn Nam luôn lấy sự uy tín và tín nhiệm của khách hàng là mục tiêu để phấn đấu, vì thế đến với đơn vị chúng tôi khách hàng nào cũng luôn cảm thấy vô cùng yên tâm và hài lòng.

Items.Title: 什么是火力基地？ | What is Firebase ?
Items.Description:
库纳尔阿格拉瓦尔

发表于这篇文章只适合初学者

让我们将这些服务分为三个不同的部分。

在开始之前，本文中的“app”表示“web 应用”，例如 pwa、react、angular、“flutter app”、“android app”，具体取决于您的项目。 构建服务包含实时数据库、Firestore、身份验证、ML、存储、云功能、应用检查等服务。

在上图中，您可以看到，您可以使用 Google、Facebook、Twitter、Git-Hub 对用户进行身份验证，也可以为用户创建自己的身份验证。

首先，您需要了解诸如集合、子集合、文档、字段、数据类型之类的术语。

文档- 它包含键值对形式的数据，键可以视为字段名称，值可以视为数据。
集合- 它包含通常具有相同结构的多个文档，每个文档都有自己的唯一 ID。
子集合 - 文档内的集合称为子集合。

数据类型- Firestore 包含一些预定义的数据类型，以便它可以更快地执行查询，例如时间戳、地理点、参考，如果您使用的是 typescript，您还可以从“firebase/firestore”获取接口
在这里，您可以执行各种类型的查询，如复合查询、复合索引和单索引等索引。

同样在 Firestore 中，您可以使用安全规则，以便只有经过身份验证或所需的用户才能访问数据库。
我们将在本文后面了解有关安全规则的更多信息。

实时数据库不同于firestore，这里更新、写入和读取数据比查询或索引更重要。

Kunal Agrawal

Posted on Jul 19

This article is only for beginners

Firebase is a suite of services offered by google like
let's breakdown those services in three different sections.

Before you begin, here in this article 'app' means 'web app' like pwa, react, angular, 'flutter app', 'android app' depending upon your project. Firebase offers services in all platforms.

1. Build


Build services contains services like Realtime database, Firestore, Authentication, ML, Storage, Cloud Functions, App Check.

1. Authentication


Firebase offers lot of different ways to authenticate user in your app. In the above image you can see, you can authenticate user with Google, Facebook, Twitter, Git-Hub, and also can create your own authentication for user.

2. Database (Firestore)


Firestore is a database from google, here you can store your data in form of collections and document. "Inside collection there may be a collection or a document and a document can also contain a collection". First of all you need to understand terms like, collection, sub-collection, document, field, data type.

Document - It contains data in form of key-value pairs, key can be considered as field name and value as data.
Collection - It contains multiple document generally of same structure, each document has its own unique id.
Sub-Collection - Collection inside a document is called a sub-collection.

Data type - Firestore contains some predefined data types so that it can perform query faster, like Timestamp, geopoint, reference, if you're using typescript you can also get there interfaces from 'firebase/firestore'
Firebase node package completely supports typescript.

Here you can perform various types of query like compound query, indexing like composite and single indexing.

Also in firestore you can use security rules, so that only authenticated or desired user can access database.
we'll learn more about security rules later in this article.

3. Realtime Database


Realtime database is different from firestore, here updating, writing and reading data is more important than querying or indexing.

Items.Title: Leetcode 挑战 #3 | Leetcode Challenge #3
Items.Description:
你好呀！ 今天我们要完成Two pointers的最后一部分。

今天的问题

今日话题： Two pointers

姓名困难问题解决方案
反向字符串简单的关联关联
反转字符串中的单词 III 简单的关联链表中间简单的关联关联
从列表末尾删除第 N 个节点中等的关联这里有 2 个字符串操作问题和 2 个链表问题。

这是一个简单的反向问题。 只需将 2 个指针同时设置在开始和结束处，交换指针处的值，然后将它们向内移动。 重复该过程直到left >= right 。

复杂性是O(n)时间和O(1)额外空间。

注意：问题是要求就地反转，而 JavaScript 不支持就地字符串操作。 所以 leetcode 发送 char 数组作为输入。 如此甜蜜！

这个问题不需要就地反向，所以是时候滥用高级语言的力量了。 哈哈！
只需要一行。 将它与地图一起使用来操作每个段。

 return s . split ( ' ' ). map ( x => x . split ( '' ). reverse (). join ( '' )). join ( ' ' );

复杂性是O(n)时间和O(n)额外空间。

注意：在这个问题中O(1)额外的空间是不可能的，因为这个问题需要一个新的字符串作为返回而不是 char 数组。 并且 JS 不支持就地字符串操作。

一个简单的解决方案是遍历链表一次以获得长度。 并再次迭代一半长度以获得中间。

另一种解决方案是保持两个指针都从头部开始。 在每次迭代中，指针 A 移动 2 步，而指针 B 移动 1 步。 这样，当指针 A 到达末尾时，指针 B 就在链表的中间。

两种解决方案都需要O(n)时间和O(1)额外空间。

与上一个问题相同，一个简单的解决方案是先计算长度。 并再次迭代以从末尾获取第 n 个节点。

另一种方法是在开始时设置指针 A 并将指针 B 设置在前面 n 步。 然后同时移动它们，当指针 B 到达末尾时，指针 A 位于从末尾开始的第 n 个节点处。

两种解决方案都需要O(n)时间和O(1)额外空间。

注意：对于第二种解决方案，请注意一些特殊情况，例如：删除第一个元素（因此我们必须将第二个元素作为头返回而不是删除的头）。

附加问题

姓名困难问题解决方案
添加两个数字中等的关联关联

我忘了设置过滤tag:two pointers ，所以额外的问题不是关于两个指针。

问题只是按照我们在小学时所学的方式来实现整数加法。

从最右边的数字开始，将它们相加，记录模和并进位，在下一个数字中重复该过程。

还有两件事需要小心处理。 这些数字的长度可能不同，不要忘记添加最后一个进位。

复杂性是O(n)时间和O(n)空间。

结论

今天的5个问题比上次简单。 完成它们只花了大约 50 分钟。
下一次，将有3个中等问题。 希望您阅读起来会更有趣。

Hi there! Today we are going to finish the last part of Two pointers.

Today's problems

Today's topic: Two pointers

ID Name Difficulty Problem Solution
344 Reverse String easy link link
557 Reverse Words in a String III easy link link
876 Middle of the Linked List easy link link
19 Remove Nth Node From End of List medium link link

There are 2 string manipulation problems and 2 linked list problems here.

344. Reverse String

This is a simple reverse problem. Just set the 2 pointers at the begining and end at the same time, swap the values at the pointers, and then move them inward. Repeat the process until left >= right.

The complexities are O(n) time and O(1) extra space.

NOTE: The problem was asking for in-place reverse, while JavaScript does not support in-place string manipulation. So leetcode sends char array as input. So sweet!

577. Reverse Words in a String III

In-place reverse is not required for this problem, so it's time to abuse the power of high-level language. LOL!
Only one line is needed. The split+join combo is a powerful tool dealing with string manipulation. Use it along with map to manipulate each segment.

return s.split(' ').map(x => x.split('').reverse().join('')).join(' ');

The complexities are O(n) time and O(n) extra space.

NOTE: O(1) extra space is impossible in this problem since the problem requires a new string as return instead of char array. And JS does not support in-place string manipulation.

876. Middle of the Linked List

A simple solution is to iterate through the linked list once to get the length. And iterate again for half of lengh to get the middle.

Another solution is maintain two pointers both starts at the head. In each iteration, the pointer A moves 2 steps while the pointer B move 1 step. This way, when pointer A reaches the end, pointer B is at the middle of the linked list.

Both solutions take O(n) time and O(1) extra space.

19. Remove Nth Node From End of List

Same as the last problem, a simple solution is to count the length first. And iterate again to get the n-th node from the end.

Another way is to set pointer A at the begining and set pointer B n step(s) ahead. Then move both of them at the same time, while pointer B reaches the end, pointer A is at the n-th node from the end.

Both solutions take O(n) time and O(1) extra space.

NOTE: For the second solution, be careful with some special cases like: removing the first element (so we have to return the second element as head instead of the removed head).

Additional Problem

ID Name Difficulty Problem Solution
2 Add two numbers medium link link

I forgot to set the filter tag:two pointers, so the additional problem is not about two pointers.

The problem is simply implementing the integer addition the way we've been taught in elementary school.

Starts from the right most digit, add them up, record the modulo sum and carry, repeat the process in the next digit.

There are two more things need to handle carefully. The numbers may not be in same length and don't forget to add the last carry.

The complexities are O(n) time and O(n) space.

Conclusion

The 5 problems today are simpler than the last time. It only took about 50 min to finish them.
Next time, there will be 3 medium problems. Hope it will be more interesting for you to read.

Items.Title: 快速分析数据的4个关键 | 4 keys of analyzing data fast
Items.Description:
哈维桑塔纳

发表于不要存储不需要的数据。 听起来很傻，但是你必须阅读的很多数据没有用。

  • 不要读取你不需要的数据。 使用索引或您的数据库/框架提供的任何其他工具丢弃数据

  • 首先运行繁重的操作。 例如，过滤数据比聚合数据要快，所以在处理数据时总是先过滤，然后再做其他繁重的事情（连接、聚合等）

  • 在存储之前对数据进行排序。 对数据进行排序可以使压缩更好，并且您可以使用当前硬件的所有功能（顺序读取比随机访问快 100 倍）

    • 遵循这 3 条规则，我处理大型数据集的速度比平时快 100-1000 倍。

    一旦暂停，javisantana 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

    一旦解除暂停，javisantana 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，javisantana 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 javisantana 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



    原文:

    javi santana

    Posted on Jul 19

    1. Don't store the data you don't need. Sounds silly but a lot of the data you have to read is not useful.

    2. Don't read the data you don't need. Discard the data using indices or any other tool your database/framework provides

    3. Run heavy operations first. For example, filtering data is faster than aggregating it so when processing data always filter first and do other heavy things later (joins, aggregations and so on)

    4. Sort your data before storing it. Sorting data makes compression much better and you use all the power of current hardware (sequential reads are 100x faster than random access)

    Following these 3 rules I process large datasets 100-1000x faster than I usually did.
